position: 2-byte opcode field at start of TFTP packet header
explanation: Numeric packet type code that directly identifies the kind of response (e.g., RRQ, WRQ, DATA, ACK, ERROR, OACK). Used to distinguish response/packet types.

position: 2-byte error code field inside an ERROR (opcode=5) packet, following the opcode
explanation: Numeric code indicating the specific error/result condition when an ERROR packet is sent; distinguishes different error response types.
